questions.statement:
<p><strong>Fill in the blanks with the most appropriate idiom for the following context.</strong></p>

<p>While it made Lawrence cry, Jack assured everyone that he was just trying to ___________.</p>
options: Put the cart before the horse /

questions.statement:
<p><strong><span style="font-weight: 700; ">DIRECTION -</span><span style="">&nbsp;Out of the given alternatives choose the one which is nearest in meaning to the given words:</span></strong></p>

<p><span style="">VILIFY&nbsp;</span></p>

<p><strong>Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word.</strong></p>
RESOLUTION

<p><span style="  font-weight: 700; white-space-collapse: preserve;">Directions: Item in this section consists of a sentence with an underlined word followed by four words. Select the option that is nearest in meaning to the underlined word and mark your response in your Answer Sheet accordingly.</span></p>

<p>She <u>despised</u> herself for being selfish.</p>

<p><strong>Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word.</strong></p>

<p>PERNICIOUS</p>

<p><strong>Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the underlined group of words.</strong></p>

<p>How did you become interested in learning <u>the art of producing beautiful handwriting</u>?&nbsp;&nbsp;</p>

<p><strong>Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.</strong></p>

<p>Flexible&nbsp;</p>

<p><strong>Select the most appropriate antonym of the given word.</strong></p>
FIDELITY

<p>Choose the option that substitutes the given phrase appropriately.</p>

<p>The time after twilight and before night</p>

<p>Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom</p>

<p>Go down like a lead balloon&nbsp;</p>
options: Put yourself in a tough position in order to support someone / something /
To be received badly by an audience /

questions.statement:
<p><strong>Fill in the blanks with the most appropriate idiom for the following context.</strong></p>

<p>In order to get the party started, Stacy decided to play an ice-breaker game ________________.</p>
options: To make a long story short /
To let someone off the hook /

questions.statement:
<p><strong>Select the word which is opposite in meaning to the word in capitals.</strong></p>
SUSPEND
questions.explanation:
<p><strong>Suspend </strong>is to stop/prevent something from continuing. So, <strong>continue </strong>is the word which is opposite in meaning to suspend.</p>

<p>Hence,&nbsp;<strong>option 2 is correct.</strong></p>

questions.statement:
<p>Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom.</p>

<p>Kill two birds with one stone</p>
options: <span style="">Face several problems at a time</span> /
<span style="">To achieve two things by doing a single action</span> /

questions.statement:
<p><strong>Select the most appropriate word for the given group of words.</strong></p>
A person who can use both hands with equal ease

<p><strong>Select the most appropriate word for the given group of words.</strong></p>

<p>A person incapable of making mistakes</p>

<p><strong>What is the antonym of the word given below?</strong></p>

<p>Initiate</p>

<p><strong>Select the most appropriate meaning of the underlined idiom.</strong></p>
Rahul is <strong><u>a great hand</u> </strong>at organizing parties.

<p><strong>Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.</strong></p>

<p>Solitude</p>

<p><strong>Fill in the blanks with the most appropriate idiom for the following context.</strong></p>

<p>______________, Tom decided that he would win the competition. He was truly determined!</p>

<p>Select the most appropriate idiom to fill in the blank.</p>
Rani has ________ about the sanitation in the colony. She can&rsquo;t stop thinking about it.

<p><strong>Select the word which is opposite in meaning to the word in capitals.</strong></p>

<p>FUSION</p>

<p>Choose the word that means the same as the given word.</p>

<p>Soar</p>

<p><strong>Fill in the blanks with the most appropriate idiom for the following context.</strong></p>

<p>No matter how hard Matt tried to be different from his brother, they were, ultimately _____________________.</p>

<p>Select the most appropriate idiom to fill in the blank.</p>
It is good to ________ with the latest developments in your field.
